title: Chris,
content:
Chris,

I’m writing with an invitation that is both deeply personal and professionally concrete. I’m developing a performance system—the Omni-Dromenon-Engine—and I need you as a narrative co-architect. Not as a commissioned artist executing a brief, but as an intellectual partner who helps me understand how to think about and collaborate with writers and storytellers for years to come.

First, context: 
--produced roughly 70 pages of documented framework across 47+ project files—philosophical treatises, technical white papers, strategic analyses, grant research, precedent studies, case analyses, and architectural documentation. 
--built a functioning proof-of-concept prototype with validated sub-100ms latency, a complete Node.js server infrastructure, audience and performer interfaces, real-time consensus algorithms, and benchmark tools.
--sophisticated GitHub ecosystem with 11 modular repositories and professional infrastructure. 

The technical work is substantial and real.

But here’s the honest part: I’ve never released anything. Not a single work to the public. Not an actual performance. Not a publication. I generate constantly but in a void. You, meanwhile, have published consistently across Baltimore Review, AGNI, Passages North, Bridport Prize, Sweet Lit, and others. You hold narrative with precision. You know how to finish. You were the creative sinews holding that entire Fort Lauderdale theatrical showcase together last year—the piece I missed but heard echoed through mutual friends—and you made it work. You understand how multiple disparate narrative threads cohere under unified dramaturgical intelligence.
